Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Site Inspection, And Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JUDITH BRASS, AND TRUSTEE OF THE JUDITH M. BRASS REVOCABLE TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 25, 2012 Motion To Compel Further Site Inspection, And Sanctions.

Defendant Judith Brass's Motion To Compel Further Site Inspection And For Sanctions is GRANTED.

On February 3, 2025 Defendant Judith M. Brass issued a demand for inspection of property to Plaintiff Frederic Hutchins Moll, for the home located at 3746 Clay Street. The inspection was to include the alleged damage to the "interior and exterior" of the premises. Plaintiff failed to timely object. The parties agreed the inspection was to occur on February 26, 2025. On February 19, 2025, Plaintiff testified at deposition that he had seen cracks in the kitchen and dining room and had noticed other cracks in the house but could not be specific as to which rooms. On the date of inspection, Plaintiff limited the inspection to the garage. Plaintiff refused access to the interior of the home, despite agreeing without objection, to the inspection of the alleged interior damage.

Defendant's meet and confer, which consisted of counsel pleading with Plaintiff to allow interior access on the inspection date and Plaintiff telling him to file a motion, was reasonable in light of the record as to this discovery and the impending trial date. Further efforts to meet and confer would be futile.

The request at issue is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Defendant Judith M. Brass has shown Plaintiff Frederic Hutchins Moll's site inspection was incomplete.

Plaintiff has not demonstrated that any objection was timely and meritorious.

The court orders Plaintiff to comply with a further site inspection of the interior of the home. Plaintiff shall provide access on or before April 25, 2025 at 12:00 p.m. to the interior of the home located at 3746 Clay Street, for site inspection of the kitchen and dining room and any other interior area of the home in which Plaintiff alleges damage.

Plaintiff has not demonstrated that his actions were substantially justified. Plaintiff shall pay to Defendant $4,000 in sanctions within 10 days of notice of order.

Counsel for the Defendat/Moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and drop a hard paper copy in Dept. 302's inbox for the court's review and signature.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
